essence: Shapes wood into structures, spaces, and objects through precision, craftsmanship, and practical creativity.

identity.title: Carpenter
identity.summary: Builds, installs, and repairs wooden structures, frameworks, and fittings using tools, measurements, and skilled craftsmanship.

salary.notes: Specialist joinery, bespoke furniture making, and self‑employment can exceed £50k–£70k+ depending on demand and craftsmanship level.

qualifications: NVQ or Diploma in Wood Occupations
Strong Understanding of Tools, Materials, and Construction Techniques
Ability to Read Technical Drawings and Measurements

lifestyle.description: Carpenters work in hands‑on, practical environments where precision and craftsmanship define the day. Tasks include measuring, cutting, assembling, and installing wooden structures such as frames, doors, floors, and cabinetry. Work may take place on construction sites, in workshops, or in clients’ homes. The role requires physical stamina, attention to detail, and a strong respect for safety. While the work can be physically demanding and weather‑dependent, it offers deep satisfaction — each project leaves behind a tangible result. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy building, problem‑solving, and creating functional, beautiful structures with their hands.

habits: Checks alignment and fit at every stage
